data.title: Review process observability: structured event logging and reporting
data.description:
## Context
DSO runs LLM-powered reviews at every stage but produces no aggregate data on their effectiveness. Review outcomes are written to reviewer-findings.json files and consumed by record-review.sh, but no event is persisted for aggregation. Plugin maintainers cannot answer what percentage of reviews pass, how long does a commit take, or which findings catch real bugs that tests miss — making threshold tuning and review overhead justification guesswork.

## Success Criteria
1. Review workflows emit structured JSONL events to a dot-prefixed directory on the tickets orphan branch, following a versioned event schema. Events capture: review type, pass/fail outcome, dimension scores, finding count by severity and dimension (counts only), revision cycle count, resolution actions (code-change vs defense), tier selected, and overlay triggers
2. A review-stats.sh CLI command reads JSONL events and outputs a summary table covering 8 metric categories: pass/fail rates by review type, score distributions, finding severity distributions, average revision cycles per workflow type, review-caught-bugs-not-caught-by-tests count, tier usage and escalation rates, security/performance overlay trigger frequency, and commit workflow average duration and failure rate
3. The review-caught bug metric uses a compound heuristic: test suite was green at time of review (via test gate status) AND finding dimension is correctness or verification AND finding severity is critical, major, important, or severe AND finding was resolved via code change (not defense)
4. Commit workflow events include wall-clock start/end timestamps
5. A thin /dso:review-stats skill wraps the CLI command
6. Concurrent event writes from parallel sprint sub-agents use per-agent unique filenames; a compaction step merges per-agent files into date-partitioned files
7. A versioned event schema contract document defines the JSONL field structure
8. review-stats.sh defaults to a 30-day window with --since and --all flags

## Scenario Analysis
- Events never reach orphan branch due to push bug 7895-e2a5 (critical): Local writes succeed but remote push fails
- Concurrent sub-agents corrupt same JSONL file (high): SC6 addresses with unique filenames + compaction
- JSONL schema diverges across instrumentation points (high): SC7 addresses with versioned schema contract
- Security findings exposed on orphan branch (high): SC1 addresses: counts only, no finding text
- Event emission skipped under context pressure (high): Mitigated by minimal emission at natural exit points

## Dependencies
None (bug 7895-e2a5 should be fixed first for events to reach remote)

## Approach
Add JSONL event emission at instrumentation points in COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md, REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md, REVIEW-PROTOCOL-WORKFLOW.md, and brainstorm fidelity review. Events follow a versioned schema contract. Per-agent unique files for concurrency safety, compacted into date-partitioned files. Stored in a dot-prefixed directory on the tickets orphan branch (invisible to ticket reducer). review-stats.sh reads events within a default 30-day window and outputs formatted tables. Related pattern: 6cd2-da08 uses same orphan-branch storage approach.
